forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KWQC25DQYWTVRA16TKYPWKCW
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8680'
+title: Migrate gated integration tests to the selection tier
+---
+## What
+Update `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests` (gated behind `MULTITOOL_INTEGRATION`) for the registry-backed findAPIs path.
+
+- `PrefixReuseTests.swift`: currently constructs the production `Librarian(surface:, librarian: profile.flash)` and asserts the second `findAPIs` call is not slower than the first (the empirical plan.md Finding #6 prefix-reuse pin). Rewrite over the production wiring: build the same `MetadataSearcher<APISurface.Entry>(mode: .selection, selection:)` that `MultiToolAgent`'s init builds (extract that construction into an internal factory in `MultiToolAgent.swift` if needed so the test and production share one code path), call `search(intent:limit:)` twice, keep the timing assertion. This pins that the registry's cached-root + fork-per-call contract holds end-to-end through *this* package's wiring on real hardware.
+- `SearchThenCallTests.swift` + `Support/ScenarioRunner.swift`: `expectedFoundAPINames` assertions currently read `foundAPIs(...).functions.map(\.name)` from flash-slot transcript events. Switch to the migrated `TranscriptAnalyzer` Selection decoding and assert on selected ids (entry paths, e.g. `{"tripCities", "weather"}`). Scenario semantics (findAPIs-precedes-runCode, invoked tool paths, repair bounds) are unchanged.
+- `Support/IntegrationGate.swift`: update the stale "drive a `Librarian` directly" comment.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] No `Librarian`/`FoundAPI` references remain under `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests`.
+- [ ] Ungated `swift test` (integration tests skipped but compiled) is green — the integration target must build.
+- [ ] With `MULTITOOL_INTEGRATION` set on capable hardware, the suite passes: prefix-reuse timing holds and scenario 3 selects `tripCities` + `weather` among ~20 distractors.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] The rewritten gated tests themselves are the tests: `MULTITOOL_INTEGRATION=1 swift test --filter FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests` passes on GPU hardware; plain `swift test` compiles and skips them.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — rewrite the assertions first (compile-failing), then the support wiring to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,22 +3,26 @@
 - 01KWQC25DQYWTVRA16TKYPWKCW
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8680'
-title: Migrate gated integration tests to the selection tier
+title: Run and fix the gated integration suite on real hardware
 ---
 ## What
-Update `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests` (gated behind `MULTITOOL_INTEGRATION`) for the registry-backed findAPIs path.
+The compile-level migration of `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests` (PrefixReuseTests over the production `MetadataSearcher`, ScenarioRunner/SearchThenCallTests over Selection ids) lands in the preceding cleanup task. This task is the gated *execution* pass on GPU hardware: run the migrated suite against real models and fix what only a live run can surface.

-- `PrefixReuseTests.swift`: currently constructs the production `Librarian(surface:, librarian: profile.flash)` and asserts the second `findAPIs` call is not slower than the first (the empirical plan.md Finding #6 prefix-reuse pin). Rewrite over the production wiring: build the same `MetadataSearcher<APISurface.Entry>(mode: .selection, selection:)` that `MultiToolAgent`'s init builds (extract that construction into an internal factory in `MultiToolAgent.swift` if needed so the test and production share one code path), call `search(intent:limit:)` twice, keep the timing assertion. This pins that the registry's cached-root + fork-per-call contract holds end-to-end through *this* package's wiring on real hardware.
-- `SearchThenCallTests.swift` + `Support/ScenarioRunner.swift`: `expectedFoundAPINames` assertions currently read `foundAPIs(...).functions.map(\.name)` from flash-slot transcript events. Switch to the migrated `TranscriptAnalyzer` Selection decoding and assert on selected ids (entry paths, e.g. `{"tripCities", "weather"}`). Scenario semantics (findAPIs-precedes-runCode, invoked tool paths, repair bounds) are unchanged.
-- `Support/IntegrationGate.swift`: update the stale "drive a `Librarian` directly" comment.
+- Run `MULTITOOL_INTEGRATION=1 swift test --filter FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests` on capable hardware.
+- Verify and, if needed, fix:
+  - **Prefix reuse still holds end-to-end through this package's wiring:** `PrefixReuseTests`' second `search(intent:limit:)` call (fork-inherited prefix) is not slower than the first (cold prefill) — the empirical plan.md Finding #6 pin, now exercised through the registry's cached-root + fork-per-call path.
+  - **Selection accuracy:** scenario 3 selects `tripCities` + `weather` among ~20 distractors, now as grammar-constrained ids rather than generated `FoundAPIs` — assert via the Selection-decoding `TranscriptAnalyzer`.
+  - Scenario semantics unchanged: findAPIs-precedes-runCode, exact invoked `tools.*` paths, repair-turn bounds.
+- Real-model behavior may need tuning (e.g. the selection preamble via `SelectionConfig.preamble`, or the search `limit`); make such adjustments here with the gated suite as the arbiter.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] No `Librarian`/`FoundAPI` references remain under `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests`.
-- [ ] Ungated `swift test` (integration tests skipped but compiled) is green — the integration target must build.
-- [ ] With `MULTITOOL_INTEGRATION` set on capable hardware, the suite passes: prefix-reuse timing holds and scenario 3 selects `tripCities` + `weather` among ~20 distractors.
+- [ ] `MULTITOOL_INTEGRATION=1 swift test --filter FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests` passes on GPU hardware.
+- [ ] Plain (ungated) `swift test` remains green.
+- [ ] No `Librarian`/`FoundAPI` identifier references exist under `Tests/FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests` (carried over from the cleanup task; re-verified here).

 ## Tests
-- [ ] The rewritten gated tests themselves are the tests: `MULTITOOL_INTEGRATION=1 swift test --filter FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests` passes on GPU hardware; plain `swift test` compiles and skips them.
+- [ ] The gated suite itself: `MULTITOOL_INTEGRATION=1 swift test --filter FoundationModelsMultitoolIntegrationTests` — all scenarios pass.
+- [ ] `swift test` (ungated) — full suite green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — rewrite the assertions first (compile-failing), then the support wiring to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — the gated suite is the failing-first signal; fix until green.
\ No newline at end of file
